member.name: T:TerraTechETCUtil.ActiveGameInterop
member.summary:
Allows TerraTech to interact with the TerraTech Mod Utility in UnityEditor.
This is all done on the same computer. There is no networking involved.

Do NOT Call while calling BlockMan or spawner blocks or the game will break!

Place this so it is flat on the ground
Spawn this fully charged and bubbled-up

Randomize the skins.
See
for more information on the skinning algorithm

Spawn even when blocks are missing!
Missing/loose blocks will not be spawned!!!

member.name: M:TerraTechETCUtil.RawTechBase.FindProperRootBlockExternal(BlockManager)
member.summary:
Mandatory for techs that have plans to be built over time by the building AI.
The first block being an anchored block will determine if the entire techs live or not on spawning. If this fails, there's a good chance the AI could have wasted money on it
member.param: The list of blocks to find the new root in

member.name: M:TerraTechETCUtil.RawTechBase.TechToMemoryExternal(Tank)
member.summary:
Mandatory for techs that have plans to be built over time by the building AI.
Since the first block placed ultimately determines the base rotation of the Tech (Arrow shown on Radar/minimap) we must be ABSOLUTELY SURE to build the Tech in relation to that first block.
Any alteration on the first block's rotation will have severe consequences in the long run.
Split techs on the other hand are mostly free from this issue.
member.param: The tech to gather data from
member.returns: A new with only the block positioning data of the Tech

member.name: M:TerraTechETCUtil.RawTechBase.TechToBlocksExternal(Tank,System.Collections.Generic.List{TerraTechETCUtil.RawBlock})
member.summary:
Mandatory for techs that have plans to be built over time by the building AI.
Since the first block placed ultimately determines the base rotation of the Tech (Arrow shown on Radar/minimap) we must be ABSOLUTELY SURE to build the Tech in relation to that first block.
Any alteration on the first block's rotation will have severe consequences in the long run.
Split techs on the other hand are mostly free from this issue.
member.param: The tech to gather data from

Fills the given with only the block positioning data of the Tech.
Must not be null
member.exception: is null

member.name: M:TerraTechETCUtil.AltUI.StartUI(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary:
Change the UI to a softer hybrid style of mostly current and some old.
Make sure to finish it with EndUI()
Use a try-finally block to insure you call EndUI() even if an exception happens!
member.param: Transparency of the window itself
Transparency of content INSIDE the window
member.exception: The UI crashed and now everything is screwed up

member.name: M:TerraTechETCUtil.AltUI.StartUIOpaque
member.summary:
Change the UI to a non-transparancy, softer hybrid style of mostly current and some old.
Make sure to finish it with EndUI()
Use a try-finally block to insure you call EndUI() even if an exception happens!
member.exception: The UI crashed and now everything is screwed up

member.summary:
Create a UI window complete with SFX and vanilla-like styling.
Automatically manages it by
Also keeps track of the cursor hovering over it so that other UI functions don't conflict with it
member.param: Unique window ID
The rect of the window, stored as a field somewhere
Window IMGUI contents call
Header title for the page
Transparency multiplier. Multiplied by
Optional Action for a close UI button
Extra IMGUI contents call for the top blue bar
Block the mouse from interacting with anything outside the window
Allow the top of this to be dragged
Additional UI options for this
member.returns: The rect of the window adjusted with window dragging
